channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Chess.com is thinking three moves ahead with its friends and family subscription
item.description: Chess.com has a new subscription option for chess families and tight-knit players: a friends and family plan.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Nonprofit Aims to Improve Online Safety with Open-Source Tools
item.description: A group of internet businesses, including Roblox and Google, have cofounded a nonprofit called ROOST to fund free, open-source tools for online safety.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government
item.description: "There are some very prominent ones that we see every day, things like at the TSA with facial recognition," said Valerie Wirtschafter. The post How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Why firewalls and VPNs give you a false sense of security
item.description: In the ever-changing landscape of cybersecurity threats, traditional pillars like firewalls and VPNs are struggling to keep pace with the evolving challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FPGAs lose their luster in the GenAI era
item.description: Intel invested $16.7 billion when it bought FPGA maker Altera in 2016, while AMD spent upwards of $35 billion in 2020 to acquire Xilinx, and but the vendors have little if anything to show for it.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Stricter lobster fishing rules scrapped after complaints from fishermen about harm to industry
item.description: Fishing industry regulators have decided to scrap stricter new lobster fishing standards off New England in the wake of months of protest from lobster fishermen that the rules were unnecessary and would bankrupt harvesters.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tech companies raise more than $27 million to build infrastructure for kids online safety
item.description: A group of leading major technology companies including OpenAI and Discord have raised more than $27 million for a new initiative focused on building open-source tools to boost online safety for kids.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ex-Treasury secretaries express worries over DOGE payment systems
item.description: Former Treasury Secretaries penned an op-ed Monday expressing their concerns that officials at Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) gained access to the federal payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Apple’s upcoming iPhone SE 4 likely to offer major improvements
item.description: IT purchasers might be interested to learn that Apple is preparing to introduce a new entry-level and more affordable next edition iPhone SE. What sets this iteration apart is that it should be powerful enough to run Apple Intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ye's X account deleted after antisemitic posts
item.description: Ye, the rapper formerly known as Kanye West, has left the social media platform X after a days-long posting spree that included multiple antisemitic comments.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Hackers are hijacking government software to access sensitive servers
item.description: A bug in Trimble Cityworks is being used to run remote code execution attacks against Microsoft IIS'.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Impact of Artificial Intelligence on White-Collar Jobs: A New Reality
item.description: A report from the Institute for Public Policy Research (IPPR) reveals that most white-collar jobs could be affected by artificial intelligence, with organizational and analytical tasks at high risk.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Is your camera ticket a fake? Here’s how to tell
item.description: Red light camera tickets: Not liable to ID the driver; some are fishing expeditions. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you fight back against tricky fake tickets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senators terrified of crossing Trump, facing Musk-funded challengers
item.description: GOP senators are terrified over the prospect of facing primary challengers funded by Elon Musk if they stick their necks out by opposing President Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: 15 most misused buzzwords in IT
item.description: CIOs constantly speak about the need to drop the technical jargon and talk “the language of business.” Yet, they don’t except business to do the same.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: A 5-point checklist before you select and implement an AI agent platform
item.description: As a CIO, it’s likely that this year, your IT team is moving quickly from evaluating and deploying discrete third-party AI software packages, to integrating custom AI agents throughout your client-facing and internal business applications for even further automation and productivity.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Seguros Atocha deja atrás un enfoque tradicional apoyándose en la digitalización
item.description: La digitalización está redefiniendo la forma en la que las organizaciones de todo el mundo gestionan sus operaciones y se relacionan con sus clientes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Notre Dame scores big with Wi-Fi 6E stadium upgrade
item.description: When 80,000 fans fill the University of Notre Dame’s iconic football stadium on Saturday afternoons in the fall to cheer on the Fighting Irish, there’s no shortage of high-decibel screaming. And now, thanks to a major network expansion, there’s no shortage of high-bandwidth streaming.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: I hate today's Wordle so much it might be my least favorite ever. And I bet you hate it too
item.description: Is it possible to have a phobia of a certain type of Wordle? If it is, I have it. Here's why I hate today's game so much.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: EV Winter is here. Carmakers are betting on a thaw
item.description: The move to electric vehicles is the auto industry’s biggest transformation since cars replaced horses early last century. Just about every traditional automaker is going through its own reckoning with the EV transition, one that presents huge opportunity—and existential risk.
item.dc:creator: Mike Colias, Fast Company

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Noem defends Musk’s access to personal data
item.description: Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem defended tech billionaire Elon Musk’s access to sensitive data housed within DHS’s Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA), saying he’s conducting a necessary “audit” of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Humans optional: Tomorrow's Internet of Agents is being planned today and will bypass homo sapiens almost entirely
item.description: Tomorrow's "Internet of Agents" is being planned today by Cisco and Microsoft and will bypass homo sapiens almost entirely.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation

item.title: Exponential growth but for how long: DeepSeek's meteoric rise in AI-land continues unabated, fresh Similarweb data shows
item.description: Data from Similarweb shows DeepSeek is now the third most used AI tool, but is its popularity already waning?
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: PlayStation Network outage live updates – PSN is back and compensation announced
item.description: PSN is finally back around 24 hours after it first went down. Here's what happened, and any further updates as we get them.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: Super Bowl 2025 LIVE: how to watch Eagles vs Chiefs online now, halftime show, live scores, odds, 3rd quarter
item.description: Eager to watch the Super Bowl? Here are the best streams, the cheapest prices and how to watch all four quarters live online as Patrick Mahomes' Kansas City face Philadelphia at New Orleans' Superdome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Quantum networking advances on Earth and in space
item.description: Cisco’s Quantum Summit brought together quantum computing experts last week from around academia, government, and industry and highlighted the progress we’ve seen over the past few months.
item.dc:creator: Network World
